sections.paragraph:
τεκνόω 1 aor. ἐτέκνωσα (Just.); aor. pass. subj. τεκνωθῶ Gen 16:2 Sym. (Hes., Fgm. 138 R.; Trag.; Phalaris, Ep. 103, 2) commonly of the man in the sense ‘beget’ (ISmyrna I [IK 23] 541, 4; Plut., Pericl. 165 [24, 10]; En 15:5; Jos., Ant. 1, 150; 2, 213; Just.), but also, and only so in our lit., of the woman bear (a child) (Jos., Ant. 4, 255) in Hb 11:11 v.l.—DELG s.v. τίκτω A. Frisk s.v. τέκνον.

aor. aor. = aorist

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

IK IK = Inschriften griechischer Städte aus Kleinasien—List 3

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Frisk Frisk = HF., Griechisches etymologisches Wörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 995
